heroHeadline: Gaza Votes in First Election in 20 Years; IDF Kills Palestinians in Jabalia as Phase 2 Ceasefire Talks Remain Deadlocked
heroSubtitle: The deadliest attack on Jewish people since the Holocaust — assault timeline, hostage crisis resolved Jan 2026, intelligence failures, and IDF response. On April 25, 2026, Gaza held its first municipal elections in nearly 20 years, with polls opening in Deir el-Balah — the first vote since Hamas won the 2006 elections and seized control of the Strip in 2007. Hamas did not field candidates but pledged non-interference; approximately 70,000 Gaza voters and ~1 million West Bank voters participated in PA-organized elections. Simultaneously, IDF forces killed multiple Palestinians in northern Gaza on Day 198 of the ceasefire: Bahjat Abu Al-Eish was shot dead in Jabalia refugee camp and others died from Israeli fire. The cumulative death toll since October 7, 2023 reached 72,585+ killed and 172,370+ injured per Gaza Health Ministry/WAFA. Phase Two disarmament talks remain in deep impasse — three Board of Peace deadlines (Apr 11, Apr 14, Apr 19) have passed without Hamas acceptance; Cairo backchannel contacts continue with no breakthrough.
footerNote: Data sourced from IDF, Israel Police, UN OCHA, Israeli government, Reuters, AP, Haaretz, WAFA, and institutional analyses. Casualty figures are official Israeli counts; Hamas-theater figures are separately noted and contested. All 251 hostages accounted for as of January 26, 2026. Cumulative conflict dead (Oct 7, 2023 – Apr 2026): 72,585+ Palestinians per OCHA/Gaza MoH/WAFA. Ceasefire in effect since Oct 2025; 2,400+ violations documented by Gaza GMO — Palestinians killed since ceasefire: 791+ as of Apr 25, 2026. Gaza reconstruction cost: $71.4B per UN-EU-World Bank joint assessment (Apr 21, 2026).
